task: A project is late because reviews take too long. Is the better first intervention clearer review ownership or adding more meetings?
If a system must minimize blast radius and the team is small, should they prefer a monolith or microservices as the starting architecture?
A bug appears only after a cache warm-up and disappears when logging is enabled. Is the likelier issue deterministic logic or a race condition?
For a one-way door decision with high uncertainty, should the team optimize for reversibility or immediate local efficiency?
If users are confused by too many options, is the better intervention more documentation or fewer defaults exposed initially?
A model is accurate but too slow for interactive use. Should the first optimization target retrieval quality or latency bottlenecks?
